jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Opening credits sequence with Homer's signature crash
jokes.punchline: Homer's iconic frustrated exclamation after physical mishap
jokes.explanation: Homer's signature catchphrase, perfectly integrated character moment but routine in context

jokes.text: Can't a man walk down the street without being offered a job?
jokes.setup: Smithers being aggressively recruited
jokes.punchline: Complaining about having too many job opportunities
jokes.explanation: Clever reversal of typical job-hunting complaints, well-delivered

jokes.text: Here we are. Ten years' experience. His name is-
jokes.setup: Recruiters consulting their files to find the next candidate
jokes.punchline: Cut to Homer already announcing he got the job before they finish reading his name
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing with the scene cut - Homer's impulsiveness undermines corporate procedure

jokes.text: I get more money, plus health benefits for me and my life partner.
jokes.setup: Homer excitedly describing job benefits
jokes.punchline: Homer referring to Marge as his 'life partner' using corporate jargon
jokes.explanation: Homer mindlessly parroting corporate speak, solid character beat

jokes.text: You took a new job in a strange town without discussing it with your family? Of course not. I wouldn't do that. Why not?
jokes.setup: Marge's reasonable concern about Homer's unilateral decision
jokes.punchline: Homer's completely honest admission that he saw nothing wrong with it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - starts to defend himself then immediately undermines it

jokes.text: I always wanted to own the Dallas Cowboys. [Laughing]
jokes.setup: Homer's 'lifelong dream' buildup with promise not to laugh
jokes.punchline: Completely unrealistic dream of owning NFL team, Lisa's immediate laughter
jokes.explanation: Great setup with the promise not to laugh, absurd dream perfectly suits Homer

jokes.text: I'm sorry, Dad. I just find that very cute.
jokes.setup: Lisa laughing at Homer's Cowboys dream
jokes.punchline: Her patronizing 'cute' dismissal
jokes.explanation: Lisa's condescending intelligence showing through, well-characterized

jokes.text: Just bring the rut with ya, honey.
jokes.setup: Marge saying she's in a happy rut and doesn't want to leave
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal solution to pack up the metaphorical rut
jokes.explanation: Homer's literal interpretation of metaphor, classic character trait

jokes.text: Uh, let's watch something else. Homer, you're trying to talk us into moving to this place. Oh, yeah, that's right.
jokes.setup: Homer starting to change channel on promotional video
jokes.punchline: Homer forgetting he's supposed to be convincing them
jokes.explanation: Homer's absent-mindedness undermining his own goals

jokes.text: Your dreams may vary from those of Globex Corporation, its subsidiaries and shareholders.
jokes.setup: Utopian corporate promotional video
jokes.punchline: Legal disclaimer undermining the fantasy
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of corporate marketing double-speak and legal disclaimers

jokes.text: did you notice how the people weren't shoving or knocking each other down? I've never been to a place like that before. Ow! Me neither.
jokes.setup: Commenting on civilized behavior in Cypress Creek
jokes.punchline: Immediately gets hurt while talking about non-violent place
jokes.explanation: Good ironic timing - violence occurs while praising non-violence

jokes.text: Oh, wow! Windows! [Chuckles]
jokes.setup: Family touring nice new house
jokes.punchline: Homer's amazement at basic architectural feature
jokes.explanation: Shows how low Homer's standards are, simple but effective

jokes.text: I would like to use your rest room, flip through your magazines, rearrange your carefully shelved items and handle your food products in an unsanitary manner.
jokes.setup: Apu saying he's not interested in buying the house
jokes.punchline: Detailed list of exactly what annoying customers do at his store
jokes.explanation: Brilliant revenge fantasy - Apu gets to turn the tables on annoying customers. Perfect character integration and escalating specificity makes it memorable.

jokes.text: Ha! Now you know how it feels! Thank you. Come again.
jokes.setup: Apu's elaborate customer revenge
jokes.punchline: His satisfied reaction and signature catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Payoff to previous joke with signature Apu catchphrase

jokes.text: [Both] Hey!
jokes.setup: Can't afford mortgage even after selling house
jokes.punchline: Simultaneous realization/exclamation from both
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat, functional but unremarkable

jokes.text: Okilly-dokilly! Bye-didilly-eye!
jokes.setup: Ned saying goodbye to the Simpsons
jokes.punchline: His characteristic made-up Flanders-speak
jokes.explanation: Classic Flanders linguistic quirk, perfectly in character

jokes.text: This town's been awfully good to us. No, it hasn't, Dad. That's why we're leaving. Oh, yeah. So long, Stinktown!
jokes.setup: Homer's sentimental goodbye to Springfield
jokes.punchline: Bart's correction followed by Homer's immediate 180-degree turn
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - Homer's false sentiment immediately abandoned when corrected

jokes.text: one of these giant redwood trees can provide enough sawdust to cover an entire day's worth of vomit at Disneyland.
jokes.setup: Lisa reading nature facts while driving
jokes.punchline: Unexpectedly gross measurement unit for tree sawdust
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and unexpected way to measure sawdust - subverts wholesome nature education

jokes.text: I call this room.
jokes.setup: Family entering large new house
jokes.punchline: Bart claiming entire huge room immediately
jokes.explanation: Simple kid humor about claiming space, works but unremarkable

jokes.text: Try the papayas. They're juicy and full of papain. Makes you strong like Popeye. Popeye, papain. See? Same thing. Uh, forget it.
jokes.setup: Hank offering papayas and making connection
jokes.punchline: His wordplay falling flat and giving up mid-explanation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Hank's enthusiasm crashing into bad wordplay

jokes.text: Don't call me that word. I don't like things that elevate me above the other people. I'm just like you. Sure, I come later in the day, I get paid a lot more and I take longer vacations, but I don't like the word 'boss.'
jokes.setup: Hank rejecting the word 'boss' as too hierarchical
jokes.punchline: Immediately listing all the ways he's actually superior
jokes.explanation: Perfect satirical contradiction - fake egalitarianism immediately undermined by listing privileges

jokes.text: Look at my feet. You like those moccasins? Look in your closet. There's a pair for you. Don't like 'em? Then neither do I! Get the hell outta here! Ha!
jokes.setup: Hank showing off his moccasins and giving Homer a pair
jokes.punchline: Threatening to kick Homer out if he doesn't like the shoes
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation from gift to threat, shows Hank's unhinged friendliness

jokes.text: Ever seen a guy say good-bye to a shoe? Yes, once.
jokes.setup: Hank asking rhetorical question about saying goodbye to shoes
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact confirmation he has seen this
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response - Homer's casual acknowledgment of absurd situation

jokes.text: We don't have bums. If we did, they wouldn't rush. They'd be allowed to go at their own pace.
jokes.setup: Homer expecting to get 'the bum's rush'
jokes.punchline: Hank's literal interpretation and utopian bum policy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay turning idiom literal, shows Hank's twisted progressive idealism

jokes.text: Well, better go upstairs and make sure the beds are still made.
jokes.setup: Marge finishing all housework by 9:30 AM
jokes.punchline: Having nothing to do, inventing unnecessary tasks
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's compulsive housekeeping nature

jokes.text: Don't call me Mr. Scorpion. It's Mr. Scorpio, but don't call me that either. Call me Hank.
jokes.setup: Homer mispronouncing Scorpio as Scorpion
jokes.punchline: Hank's correction then immediate rejection of formality
jokes.explanation: Shows Hank's casual management style and Homer's typical mistakes

jokes.text: At Globex, we don't believe in walls. Matter of fact, I didn't even give you my coat.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to hang up Hank's coat
jokes.punchline: Hank revealing there are no walls and he never gave Homer a coat
jokes.explanation: Surreal misdirection - sets up corporate philosophy gag then reveals Homer was confused about basic reality

jokes.text: My butt is for sitting, not for kissing.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to be a yes-man
jokes.punchline: Hank's blunt rejection of brown-nosing
jokes.explanation: Crude but effective way to establish Hank's direct communication style

jokes.text: Homer, don't give up. They laughed at me the first time I wore jeans with a sport coat. I was the first wealthy man in America to ever do that. Now they all do it!
jokes.setup: Hank encouraging Homer about his Cowboys dream
jokes.punchline: Claiming to have invented casual Friday fashion
jokes.explanation: Absurd claim to historical fashion significance, perfectly captures Hank's megalomaniacal tendencies

jokes.text: Homer, I gotta go upstairs. There's a problem. Somebody ate part of my lunch.
jokes.setup: Hank taking urgent phone call during trust exercise
jokes.punchline: The 'emergency' is someone eating his lunch
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of dramatic tension - urgent call about trivial office problem

jokes.text: You got a fresh sound. It'll play well at this school.
jokes.setup: Bart making fart noises at new school
jokes.punchline: Student treating fart sounds like music industry talent
jokes.explanation: Clever application of showbiz terminology to juvenile behavior

jokes.text: 'Cause I've been looking for someone to boss me around.
jokes.setup: Milhouse asks if Bart wants a best friend
jokes.punchline: His immediate admission of wanting to be dominated
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse character moment - eager but pathetic in his honesty about wanting submission

jokes.text: So, you never learned cursive? Uh, well, I know hell and damn and bit- Cursive handwriting. Script.
jokes.setup: Teacher asking about cursive writing skills
jokes.punchline: Bart thinking she means curse words
jokes.explanation: Classic kid misunderstanding, fits Bart's character perfectly

jokes.text: I moved here from Canada, and they think I'm slow, eh? I fell off the jungle gym, and when I woke up I was in here. I start fires.
jokes.setup: Students explaining why they're in remedial class
jokes.punchline: Escalating from misunderstanding to head injury to arson
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation from innocent to alarming, good structure building to arson reveal

jokes.text: Let me get this straight. We're behind the rest of our class and we're going to catch up to them by going slower than they are?
jokes.setup: Remedial class methodology
jokes.punchline: Bart identifying the logical flaw in remedial education
jokes.explanation: Sharp educational satire - Bart cuts through the illogic of 'remedial' approach

jokes.text: Sounds to me like someone's got a case of the 's'pose'das.'
jokes.setup: Bart complaining about being 'supposed to' be in fourth grade
jokes.punchline: Teacher's condescending made-up condition
jokes.explanation: Mildly clever wordplay showing teacher's patronizing attitude

jokes.text: Hello, Mr. Chipmunk. You're a northern reticulated chipmunk. Yes, you are. You are so reticulated.
jokes.setup: Lisa talking to wildlife in her new environment
jokes.punchline: Using 'reticulated' like it's a compliment without knowing what it means
jokes.explanation: Shows Lisa using big words without full understanding, good character moment

jokes.text: a baseball made out of Secretariat!
jokes.setup: Homer browsing sports collectibles
jokes.punchline: Baseball made from racehorse leather
jokes.explanation: Darkly absurd collectible, memorable for its disturbing specificity

jokes.text: To Berman's Dry Cleaning. Best wishes, Tom Landry.
jokes.setup: Homer excited about autographed Tom Landry hat
jokes.punchline: Generic autograph to random dry cleaner
jokes.explanation: Deflates collectible value with mundane autograph recipient

jokes.text: What you guys need is hammocks.
jokes.setup: Workers complaining about being tired
jokes.punchline: Homer's solution is workplace hammocks
jokes.explanation: Absurd management solution, perfectly Homer-like thinking

jokes.text: Hammocks? My goodness, what an idea. Why didn't I think of that? Hammocks! Homer, there's four places. There's the Hammock Hut. That's on Third. There's Hammocks-R-Us. That's on Third too. You got Put-Your-Butt-There. That's on Third. Swing Low, Sweet Chariot. That's on Third. Matter of fact, they're all in the same complex. It's the hammock complex on Third. Oh, the hammock district. That's right.
jokes.setup: Homer asking where to buy business hammocks
jokes.punchline: Elaborate description of entire hammock district with themed store names
jokes.explanation: Legendary Simpsons moment - escalating absurdity of hammock specialization, perfect structure building to 'hammock district.' Became cultural reference point.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, the 59th Street bridge! Maybe it just collapsed on its own. We can't take that chance. You always say that. I want to take a chance.
jokes.setup: Officials seeing bridge destruction from doomsday device
jokes.punchline: One official's desire to finally take a chance on bridge collapse
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing bureaucratic dynamics under pressure

jokes.text: You know, there's a little place called Mary Ann's Hammocks. The nice thing about that place is Mary Ann gets in the hammock with you. [Laughs] I'm just kidding.
jokes.setup: Hank recommending another hammock place
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate suggestion followed by admission he's joking
jokes.explanation: Mild innuendo joke, shows Hank's inappropriate humor

jokes.text: By the way, Homer, what's your least favorite country? Italy or France? France. [Chuckles] Nobody ever says Italy.
jokes.setup: Hank asking Homer's least favorite country
jokes.punchline: Casual observation about people's predictable hatred of France
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about casual xenophobia, delivered with Hank's characteristic nonchalance

jokes.text: Sugar? Sure. Here you go. Sorry it's not in packages. Want some cream?
jokes.setup: Homer asks for sugar while a villain is being interrogated
jokes.punchline: Polite hospitality during what appears to be torture
jokes.explanation: Absurd politeness in the middle of what appears to be a torture scene

jokes.text: So, do you expect me to talk? I don't expect anything from you except to die and be a very cheap funeral.
jokes.setup: James Bond-style villain interrogation
jokes.punchline: Hank's crude, cost-conscious threat
jokes.explanation: Great Bond parody - replaces elegant threat with crude financial concern

jokes.text: I tackled a loafer at work today.
jokes.setup: Homer describing his day after tackling secret agent
jokes.punchline: Casual description of taking down government spy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer obliviousness - treats major incident as minor workplace annoyance

jokes.text: Nice work, Homer! Am I proud of you! When you go home tonight, there's gonna be another story on your house.
jokes.setup: Homer accidentally helps defeat the secret agent
jokes.punchline: Hank rewards him with home improvement
jokes.explanation: Hank treats helping defeat a spy as worthy of a standard employee bonus

jokes.text: I've been so bored since we moved here I found myself drinking a glass of wine every day. I know doctors say you should drink a glass and a half, but I just can't drink that much.
jokes.setup: Marge confessing to drinking problem
jokes.punchline: Her 'problem' is drinking too little according to medical advice
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - sets up alcoholism concern then reveals Marge's typically modest 'excess'

jokes.text: I can't get in trouble at school. They put me in the remedial class. I'm surrounded by arsonists and kids with mittens pinned to their jackets all year round.
jokes.setup: Bart explaining why he can't get in trouble
jokes.punchline: Description of his harmless classmates escalating to arsonists
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier special ed class description

jokes.text: I can't get in trouble at school. They put me in the remedial class. I'm surrounded by arsonists and kids with mittens pinned to their jackets all year round.
jokes.setup: Bart explains why he can't get in trouble
jokes.punchline: Mix of dangerous kids and infantilized ones
jokes.explanation: Callback to the arsonist from earlier, combined with new detail about overprotected kids

jokes.text: My team is way ahead of the weather machine and germ warfare divisions.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his work success
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of evil corporation divisions
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Homer's obliviousness to working for supervillain organization

jokes.text: Oh, those jerks- always walking over the small businessman. Don't get me started about the government.
jokes.setup: Hank having trouble with the government
jokes.punchline: Supervillain complaining about government oppression of business
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic framing - international terrorist as oppressed small business owner

jokes.text: Let 'em go. You'll stay here with me. We'll go bowling.
jokes.setup: Homer says his family wants to leave
jokes.punchline: Hank's immediate suggestion they abandon the family for bowling
jokes.explanation: Hank's sociopathic but friendly suggestion to abandon family for recreation

jokes.text: But, Homer, on your way out, if you wanna kill somebody, it would help me a lot.
jokes.setup: Hank saying goodbye to Homer
jokes.punchline: Casual request for murder as parting favor
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly casual evil request - perfectly captures Hank's friendly supervillain character

jokes.text: Aw, the Denver Broncos! I think owning the Denver Broncos is pretty good. Yeah, yeah. Well, explain to me why it isn't. [Sighs] You just don't understand football, Marge.
jokes.setup: Homer receiving NFL team as gift
jokes.punchline: Disappointed it's not the Dallas Cowboys, dismissing Marge's logic
jokes.explanation: Good callback to opening dream, shows Homer's impossible-to-satisfy nature
